digest.component_or_area: store-level compatibility record and fail-closed capability gate
propositions: A committed store-level compatibility record (format_version + required_capabilities) must be added at a fixed path and read by a gate before any mutating or externally-publishing operation, failing closed on unknown version or capability.

The gate must handle four explicit record states: absent = legacy version 0 pass-through, present+compatible = pass, present+incompatible = fail-closed, present+corrupt/unreadable = fail-closed (not treated as absent).
A v1.0 binary must ship the reader now because an already-installed binary cannot later be taught to inspect a record whose semantics were undefined in v1.0.

The gate must be wired into all mutation/publish entry points including event append/transition/claim/delete/archive, compaction, the reconciler inbound write path, Jira push/reconcile before the outbound API call, MCP write tools, automatic repair, and fsck_recover.
The record must be written idempotently via an ensure unit using atomic tempfile-then-rename, and because run_ensures is fail-silent the covered-write path must not depend on the sweep having run.

Read-only and diagnostic operations (fsck, backup, inspection) stay available under an incompatible/corrupt record and report the unsupported version/capability; optional additive events retain preserve-and-ignore behaviour while required capabilities fail closed.
